response.justification: Candidate has strong full-stack and systems experience, plus relevant geospatial background through GISCollective and Green Map work. However, the role specifically requires modern C++ or Rust as a primary language, and the resume shows only brief C/C++ exposure from 2014-2015 at National Instruments, with no Rust experience. Primary expertise is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. The geospatial domain knowledge and low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic are valuable, but the core language requirement gap is significant for a senior IC role. Also currently on a career gap.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS experience via GISCollective and Green Map platform work
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic (millions of req/sec)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testing, Linux server applications
concerns: No modern C++ experience — only legacy C/C++ work from 10+ years ago at National Instruments
No Rust experience listed anywhere in resume or projects
Currently on a career gap (caregiving) since October 2025, affecting immediate availability
